figures.caption: Figure 1: Overall schematic. (a) Spatiotemporal DiT backbone for learning the velocity field in the sample (latent) space; (b) Scientific VAE for dimensionality reduction; (c) ViT-Transformer for loading embedding preprocessing.

Figure 2: Spatiotemporal DiT backbone. After being compressed into the latent space, each frame is patchified, and each patch is projected into a token. The frame-wise spatial tokens are then concatenated across time to form a spatiotemporal token sequence. Positional encoding and weighted token-level loading embedding are added to the tokens. The resulting sequence is processed by DiT blocks, where timestep and loading-path conditions modulate the blocks through adaLN. Finally, the processed tokens are projected back to latent patches and unpatchified to recover the original spatiotemporal latent shape.
